title: Dungeon Bathhouse Architect
description: The user takes on the role of an architect redesigning an ancient dungeon into a luxury underground bathhouse. They must position and configure a pool within the dungeon space, adjust ambient lighting to create atmosphere, set water color and material properties, rotate the dungeon orientation, scale the pool to fit the chamber, and finalize the design with a cinematic camera flythrough.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive dungeon bathhouse architecture tool. Load both GLB assets: 'shared_assets/dungeon_warkarma.glb' as the dungeon structure and 'shared_assets/pool.glb' as a pool to be placed inside it. Use GLTFLoader from three/addons. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 8, 15) looking at the origin. Add OrbitControls for default navigation. Lighting: one warm-toned PointLight (0xffaa55) above the scene, one cool AmbientLight (0x334466), and a subtle blue PointLight (0x4488ff) near the pool to simulate water glow. Create an HTML overlay panel with these interactive controls: (1) Three sliders for pool X/Y/Z position ranging -10 to 10, default 0, with 0.1 step. (2) A rotation slider for the dungeon model from 0 to 360 degrees. (3) A uniform scale slider for the pool from 0.5 to 3.0, default 1.0. (4) A color picker input to change the pool's primary material color (traverse the pool model's meshes and update material.color). (5) A range slider for the blue PointLight intensity from 0 to 5, default 1.5, labeled 'Water Glow'. (6) A 'Cinematic View' button that, when clicked, disables OrbitControls and animates the camera in a smooth circular path around the scene at radius 14 and height 6 over 5 seconds using requestAnimationFrame with elapsed time, then re-enables OrbitControls. Validate all numeric parameters: clamp positions to [-10,10], scale to [0.5,3.0], rotation to [0,360], intensity to [0,5]. Style the panel with a dark semi-transparent background, white text, positioned on the left side. Render with antialiasing enabled, toneMapping set to ACESFilmicToneMapping, and outputColorSpace set to SRGBColorSpace. Add a ground plane with MeshStandardMaterial in dark stone color (0x222222) with roughness 0.9. The animation loop should continuously render the scene.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  poolPositionX: number,         // X coordinate of pool placement in dungeon space
  poolPositionY: number,         // Y coordinate of pool placement (height)
  poolPositionZ: number,         // Z coordinate of pool placement in dungeon space
  dungeonRotationY: number,         // Y-axis rotation of dungeon structure in degrees
  poolScale: number,         // Uniform scale factor applied to pool model
  poolColorHex: string,         // Hexadecimal color code for pool material
  waterGlowIntensity: number,         // Intensity of blue point light simulating water glow
  cinematicModeActive: boolean,         // Whether cinematic camera view is currently active
  ambientLightIntensity: number,         // Intensity of ambient lighting in scene
  dungeonLoaded: boolean,         // Whether dungeon GLB asset has finished loading
  poolLoaded: boolean,         // Whether pool GLB asset has finished loading
}
